content.sanskrit: श्रोत्रस्य श्रोत्रं मनसो मनो यद्वाचो ह वाचं स उ प्राणस्य प्राणः चक्षुषश्चक्षुरतिमुच्य धीराः प्रेत्यास्माल्लोकादमृता भवन्ति ॥
content.transliteration: śrotrasya śrotraṁ manaso mano yadvāco ha vācaṁ sa u prāṇasya prāṇaḥ cakṣuṣaścakṣuratimucya dhīrāḥ pretyāsmāllokādamṛtā bhavanti ||
content.text: It is the ear of the ear, the mind of the mind, the speech of speech, the life of life, and the eye of the eye. The wise, freed from sense-perceptions, on leaving this world become immortal.

content.sanskrit: केनेषितं पतति प्रेषितं मनः केन प्राणः प्रथमः प्रैति युक्तः । केनेषितां वाचमिमां वदन्ति चक्षुः श्रोत्रं क उ देवो युनक्ति ॥
content.transliteration: keneṣitaṁ patati preṣitaṁ manaḥ kena prāṇaḥ prathamaḥ praiti yuktaḥ | keneṣitāṁ vācamimāṁ vadanti cakṣuḥ śrotraṁ ka u devo yunakti ||
content.text: By whose will directed does the mind fly forth? By whose command does the first breath move? By whose will do people utter speech? What god directs the eye and ear?

content.sanskrit: न तत्र चक्षुर्गच्छति न वाग्गच्छति नो मनो न विद्मो न विजानीमो यथैतदनुशिष्यात् ।
content.transliteration: na tatra cakṣurgacchati na vāggacchati no mano na vidmo na vijānīmo yathaitadanuśiṣyāt |
content.text: There the eye does not go, nor speech, nor the mind. We do not know, we do not understand, how anyone can teach this.

content.sanskrit: यदि मन्यसे सुवेदेति दभ्रमेवापि नूनं त्वं वेत्थ ब्रह्मणो रूपम् । यदस्य त्वं यदस्य देवेष्वथ नु मीमांस्यमेव ते मन्ये विदितम् ॥
content.transliteration: yadi manyase suvedeti dabhramevāpi nūnaṁ tvaṁ vettha brahmaṇo rūpam | yadasya tvaṁ yadasya deveṣvatha nu mīmāṁsyameva te manye viditam ||
content.text: If you think 'I know Brahman well,' then surely you know very little of Its form. What you know of Brahman in yourself, what you know in the gods — that alone is worth contemplating.

content.sanskrit: नाहं मन्ये सुवेदेति नो न वेदेति वेद च । यो नस्तद्वेद तद्वेद नो न वेदेति वेद च ॥
content.transliteration: nāhaṁ manye suvedeti no na vedeti veda ca | yo nastadveda tadveda no na vedeti veda ca ||
content.text: I do not think that I know Brahman well, nor do I think that I do not know It. He among us who knows what is meant by 'I neither know nor do I not know' — he knows Brahman.

content.sanskrit: ब्रह्म ह देवेभ्यो विजिग्ये तस्य ह ब्रह्मणो विजये देवा अमहीयन्त ।
content.transliteration: brahma ha devebhyo vijigye tasya ha brahmaṇo vijaye devā amahīyanta |
content.text: Brahman won a victory for the gods. The gods became elated in the victory of Brahman.
